parts.title: Home Economics Teachers
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] class in Home Economics that meets the [reference to current curriculum] goals for [specific focus, e.g. cooking], with three activities and a reflection.
Give me five strategies for motivating a [grade X] student who is disengaged in Home Economics cooking lessons, with one engaging idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class a basic kitchen skill (e.g. [specific skill, e.g. chopping vegetables]), with three steps and a recipe.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. sustainable consumption] into a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class, with one activity for each.
Create a list of five short encouraging phrases to build a [grade X] student's confidence during a cooking task in Home Economics.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class, with three steps and an introductory task on hygiene.
Suggest three ways to use local or seasonal ingredients in Sweden during a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class, with a recipe suggestion for each.
Write a short template for a 10-minute closing routine for a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class, with three elements (e.g. taste test, reflection).
Give me five ideas for making a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class more creative, focusing on [specific goal, e.g. meal planning], with one example for each.
Create a list of three short reflection questions for a [grade X] class after a Home Economics lesson on [specific theme, e.g. nutrition], to capture their insights.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with Science teachers at a [primary school] to connect Home Economics to [specific theme, e.g. chemistry in cooking], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's ability in [specific skill, e.g. budgeting], with three criteria.
Write a list of five resources (e.g. apps, recipes) for a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. sustainable choices].
Suggest three ways to adapt a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. allergies], with one solution for each.
Create a weekly planning template for Home Economics for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. cooking, economics, reflection).
Give me five ideas for using minimal ingredients in a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class to prepare [specific recipe, e.g. soup], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific consumer knowledge, e.g. comparing prices], with one practical exercise for each.
Write a short guide for managing a stressful situation during a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class, with three steps and a calming strategy.
Give me an example of a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class that combines cooking with [specific theme, e.g. culture], with three activities.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the school kitchen at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. sustainability], with one example for each.
Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] class in Home Economics focusing on [specific curriculum goal, e.g. household resources], with three activities and a short reflection.
Give me five ways to make cooking more enjoyable for a [grade X] class in Home Economics, with one creative idea for each that connects to their everyday lives.
Write a short guide for introducing a [grade X] class to [specific kitchen skill, e.g. kneading dough], with three steps and a simple recipe.
Suggest three ways to connect a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class to [specific theme, e.g. climate impact], with one cooking task for each.
Create a list of five short tips for a [grade X] student to succeed with [specific task, e.g. planning a meal], based on your expertise.
Give me an example of a closing routine for a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class, with three steps and an evaluation activity.
Suggest three ways to use leftovers in a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class to create [specific recipe, e.g. a gratin], with one example for each.
Write a short template for a 15-minute introduction to a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class on [specific theme, e.g. nutrition], with three key points.
Give me five ideas for integrating [specific tool, e.g. budgeting apps] into a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class, with one purpose for each.
Create a list of three short discussion questions for a [grade X] class after a Home Economics lesson on [specific topic, e.g. food waste], to reflect on their choices.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with Mathematics teachers at a [grades 7–9 school] to connect Home Economics to [specific theme, e.g. proportions in recipes], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple plan for teaching a [grade X] class to prepare a vegetarian dish over one lesson, with three steps and one learning goal.
Write a list of five simple recipes for a [grade X] class in Home Economics that reinforce [specific goal, e.g. economic thinking], with a short description of each.
Suggest three ways to support a [grade X] student who feels uncertain in the kitchen during a Home Economics lesson, with one supportive approach for each.
Create a term planning template for Home Economics for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. cooking, economics, health) and timeframes.
Give me five ideas for cooking with seasonal ingredients in a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class, with one recipe suggestion for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific consumer skill, e.g. reading ingredient labels], with one exercise for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. motor difficulties] in a Home Economics lesson, with three steps.
Give me an example of a Home Economics lesson for a [grade X] class that combines cooking with [specific theme, e.g. global food traditions], with three activities.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with parents at an [upper secondary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. healthy eating habits], with one example for each.

Suggest three ways to collaborate with different subject teachers at a [grades 7–9 school] to connect Home Economics to content that also appears in other school subjects. Give me two concrete ideas for each way of collaborating.
